examples.title: Recursive Inorder Traversal
examples.description: Standard recursive algorithm for inorder traversal of binary tree
examples.code:
PROCEDURE Inorder(node)
  IF node = NULL THEN
    RETURN
  END IF
  Inorder(node.left)    // Visit left subtree
  PRINT node.data        // Visit root
  Inorder(node.right)   // Visit right subtree
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: Recursively visits left subtree, then root, then right subtree. For BST, produces sorted output.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(h) where h is height of tree

examples.title: Recursive Preorder Traversal
examples.description: Standard recursive algorithm for preorder traversal
examples.code:
PROCEDURE Preorder(node)
  IF node = NULL THEN
    RETURN
  END IF
  PRINT node.data        // Visit root first
  Preorder(node.left)    // Then left subtree
  Preorder(node.right)   // Then right subtree
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: Root is visited before its children. Useful for creating tree copies and prefix expressions.

examples.title: Level-order Traversal
examples.description: Implementation using queue for breadth-first traversal
examples.explanation: Uses FIFO queue. Processes all nodes at current level before moving to next level. Breadth-First Search (BFS) implementation.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(w) where w is max width (worst case O(n))

examples.title: Iterative Inorder Traversal
examples.code:
PROCEDURE InorderIterative(root)
  IF root = NULL THEN
    RETURN
  END IF
  
  stack = empty stack
  current = root
  
  WHILE current != NULL OR stack is not empty DO
    WHILE current != NULL DO
      stack.push(current)    // Go to leftmost
      current = current.left
    END WHILE
    
    current = stack.pop()    // Process node
    PRINT current.data
    current = current.right  // Move to right
  END WHILE
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: Simulates recursion using explicit stack. Useful when recursion depth is a concern. Avoids call stack overflow.
